ddls:
CREATE TABLE "journeys" (
  "journey_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the journey */,
  "journey_name" TEXT /* Name of the journey */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the journey was created */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the journey */,
  "owner_id" TEXT /* Reference to the user who created the journey */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the journey (e.g., active, archived, draft) */,
  "tags" TEXT /* Tags for categorizing the journey */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("journey_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "journey_versions" (
  "version_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the version of the journey */,
  "journey_id" TEXT /* Reference to the journey this version belongs to */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the journey */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the version of the journey was created */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the journey version */,
  "created_by" TEXT /* Reference to the user who created the version */,
  "updated_date" TEXT /* Date the version was last updated */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the version is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_journey_versions_journey_id FOREIGN KEY ("journey_id") REFERENCES journeys ("journey_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "journey_steps" (
  "step_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the step */,
  "version_id" TEXT /* Reference to the version of the journey this step belongs to */,
  "step_name" TEXT /* Name of the step */,
  "step_description" TEXT /* Description of the step */,
  "step_order" TEXT /* Order of the step within the journey */,
  "action_type" TEXT /* Type of action (e.g., email, SMS, push notification) */,
  "action_details" TEXT /* Details of the action to be performed */,
  "duration" TEXT /* Duration or delay before the next step (in seconds) */,
  "condition" TEXT /* Condition that must be met for the step to execute */,
  "target_audience" TEXT /* Target audience for the step */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("step_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_journey_steps_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES journey_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "journey_performance" (
  "performance_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the performance metric */,
  "version_id" TEXT /* Reference to the version of the journey this metric belongs to */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "metric_date" TEXT /* Date the metric was recorded */,
  "metric_description" TEXT /* Description of the performance metric */,
  "user_segment" TEXT /* User segment to which the metric applies */,
  "channel" TEXT /* Channel through which the metric was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_journey_performance_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES journey_versions ("version_id")
)
